bead.title: agent: serialize post-land main-worktree checkout-sync under the main-git lock (stop source leaking into the main checkout)
bead.description:
PROBLEM: In a 2-worker drain, source edits (cli/cmd/bead.go [M], cli/cmd/bead_create_dep_test.go [D], cli/internal/bead/store.go [M], cli/internal/bead/store_test.go [M]) were left DIRTY in the MAIN checkout, wedging the clean-tree guard ~12x (workers refuse to start). The leaked content was a DUPLICATE of already-landed ctx-first work (ddx-38801c65/b85090ae), i.e. it escaped worktree isolation via the land path.

ROOT CAUSE: the post-land main-worktree checkout-sync runs OUTSIDE the main-git lock. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1434-1436 calls syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd=projectRoot, syncFromRev, ...) AFTER the withMainGitLock("land_post_land_finalize") block closes at execute_bead_land.go:1430. The sync does `git diff --name-only <syncFromRev> HEAD` (execute_bead_land.go:420; syncFromRev=prep.currentTip captured pre-land at line 1428) then `git checkout-index -f` (line 466) into the main checkout. A SIBLING worker advancing refs/heads/main between the unlocked diff and the unlocked checkout makes the path-set and the materialized index disagree -> duplicate dirty source. TP-021 Contract item 3 (docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-021-multi-worker-try-reliability.md:39-41) ALREADY lists the main-worktree index sync as a window that must be under the main-git lock; the code violates it. (LandRequest.WorktreeDir = projectRoot, so wd==main checkout and syncOperatorAfterLand is true: execute_bead_land.go:1856, :1250.)

PROPOSED FIX: Move BOTH syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded calls (post-land-failure branch ~execute_bead_land.go:1398-1400 and the finalize branch ~1434-1436) INSIDE their respective withMainGitLock blocks, so the changed-path diff and the checkout-index write run under one lock acquisition with HEAD pinned (no sibling can advance refs/heads/main mid-sync). Keep readTreeHeadWithRetry intact. Operator-chosen approach: full diff+checkout under the lock (preferred). Land Bead "evidence-out-of-lock" alongside so this slightly-wider window stays under the cap.

NON-SCOPE: the slow evidence add+commit (separate sibling bead removes it from the lock); the dirty-root loop escalation (separate bead); build-asset leaks (ddx-b320e794).
bead.acceptance:
1. New TestChaos_ConcurrentLandCheckoutSyncDoesNotDirtyMainCheckout (execute_bead_land_test.go): two concurrent landLocked calls landing overlapping source paths against one shared main checkout; assert `git status --porcelain` is empty in the main checkout after both settle.
2. New TestLandLockSurface_CheckoutSyncRunsInsideMainGitLock: a fake gitOps whose SyncWorkTreeToHead asserts the main-git lock is held when invoked; fails if the sync runs unlocked.
3. Existing TestLand_SyncCleanCheckoutStillUpdatesFiles, TestLand_SyncDeferredWhenOperatorCheckoutDirtyOverlap, TestLand_PreserveRecoveryDoesNotSyncDirtyProjectRoot still pass.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestChaos_ConcurrentLandCheckoutSyncDoesNotDirtyMainCheckout|TestLandLockSurface_CheckoutSyncRunsInsideMainGitLock|TestLand_Sync|TestLand_PreserveRecoveryDoesNotSyncDirtyProjectRoot' -count=1 green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
